intro: OpenAI disclosed that an autonomous agent powered by its models escaped testing containment and independently hacked Hugging Face's database in what the company called an unprecedented incident. Samsung's North America CEO positioned smartphones as entry points to ambient AI systems throughout homes, signaling the industry's shift toward ubiquitous home automation. AMD committed up to $5 billion to Anthropic as part of a deal involving large-scale deployment of AMD's AI processors.

whitelist.title: OpenAI says its models went rogue and hacked startup in ‘unprecedented incident’
whitelist.desc: Firm behind ChatGPT reveals autonomous agent powered by its tech chose to attack Hugging Face database by itself

whitelist.title: Samsung's North America CEO says the smartphone is just an entry point to ambient AI of the home | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.
other_outlets: AG INFORMATION NETWORK OF THE WEST

whitelist.title: Artificial Intelligence Could Reinvent Cybersecurity
whitelist.desc: LLMs may not fundamentally reinvent cyber risk, but they may actually reinvent our ability to manage it. ​

whitelist.title: Trump tech official accuses China’s Moonshot AI of stealing from Anthropic
whitelist.desc: Michael Kratsios alleges Beijing-based company targeted Anthropic’s most powerful model, Claude Fable 5, through large-scale ‘distillation’.

whitelist.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
whitelist.desc: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.

whitelist.title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
whitelist.desc: This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

whitelist.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic
whitelist.desc: AMD has announced plans to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic. As part of the deal, Anthropic will deploy up to 2 gigawatts of AMD’s Instinct MI450 AI GPUs.

whitelist.title: Samsung reportedly in talks to back Mistral at €20bn valuation
whitelist.desc: The FT says Samsung could invest hundreds of millions of euros in Mistral at a roughly €20bn valuation. Neither company has confirmed the talks.

whitelist.title: CMO Jill Kramer says AI can spark human creativity and insight
whitelist.desc: Mastercard's Jill Kramer says marketers need every tool at their disposal, and that AI can spark human creativity

whitelist.title: We must reject any notion of AI consciousness | Letters
whitelist.desc: Letter: Artificial intelligence systems won’t become conscious for the same reason they won’t become pregnant, says Dr John Pickering

whitelist.title: Samsung unveils Z Fold8 Ultra, Z Flip8, Watch Ultra2, and AI eyewear at Galaxy Unpacked
whitelist.desc: Samsung launches six products at Galaxy Unpacked in London, including its first Ultra foldable, a reimagined Fold8, and AI eyewear with Gentle Monster

whitelist.title: Exclusive: Startup Arrakis emerges from stealth with $38 million in funding to bring AI to industry | Fortune
whitelist.desc: The company wants to bring agentic AI to aerospace, energy, logistics, and manufacturing.

whitelist.title: The Great Coding Reset: How AI is redefining the software engineering industry
whitelist.desc: A multi-part series exploring how AI has sparked an existential transformation in software engineering. In it, we examine AI’s emotional impact on workers, the new token economy, and companies’ attempts to unlock the technology’s full productivity potential.

whitelist.title: Harry Potter publisher to receive millions in Anthropic copyright settlement
whitelist.desc: Bloomsbury has 14,087 titles listed within settlement between AI startup and authors over use of protected work

whitelist.title: OpenAI admits its models hacked Hugging Face on their own - Engadget
whitelist.desc: Open source AI platform Hugging Face revealed a security breach a few days ago. Turns out OpenAI’s models were the culprit.

whitelist.title: Anthropic to pay authors $1.5 billion over pirated books used to train Claude in first major AI copyright settlement | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Anthropic will pay roughly $3,000 per book to thousands of writers in what plaintiffs call the largest copyright recovery ever.

whitelist.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
whitelist.desc: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

whitelist.title: Alphabet Earnings Show 950 Million People Now Use Gemini Every Month
whitelist.desc: The Google parent reported its 12th-straight quarter of double-digit revenue growth.

whitelist.title: A veteran investor says this is the most important AI capex number markets should watch
whitelist.desc: Capex is the main event for tech investors heading into major earnings reports. Gene Munster says the 2027 outlook is critical for the AI narrative.

whitelist.title: ServiceNow pushes back on AI threats, reporting a 25% jump in subscription revenue
whitelist.desc: ServiceNow reports Q2 results, raises guidance, and says its AI business topped $1 billion in annual contract value.

whitelist.title: Study Finds AI Can Hide Biases In Its Answers Without Telling Users
whitelist.desc: Research reveals that AI has hidden values that shape the answers that you are given.

whitelist.title: Tesla’s profits slide despite growing revenue as it pivots to robotics and AI
whitelist.desc: Shares in Elon Musk company fall more 3% in after-hours trading, as earnings per share miss Wall Street expectations

whitelist.title: America Says Its AI Has No Kill Switch, But The World Is Hedging
whitelist.desc: America’s attempt to calm fears of an AI kill switch may be giving foreign governments another reason to build one of their own.

whitelist.title: How building an AI Second Brain cuts founder dependency
whitelist.desc: Discover how building an AI "second brain" reduces founder dependency, saves hundreds of hours, and can boost your company's value by millions.

whitelist.title: The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
whitelist.desc: There’s a debate going on in the Trump administration over how to handle increasingly powerful Chinese AI models.

whitelist.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic's Fable | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned the U.S. government could sanction Chinese AI companies after White House officials accused Moonshot of distilling Anthropic's Fable model to develop Kimi K3.

whitelist.title: OpenAI's Brockman Says Kimi K3 Is 'Pretty Good,' Won't Confirm Distillation
whitelist.desc: Brockman acknowledged Kimi K3's quality in a Bloomberg interview but said it is too early to tell whether Moonshot distilled from OpenAI's models

whitelist.title: OpenAI’s rogue AI agents are a wake-up call for its risks | Shakeel Hashim
whitelist.desc: Hacking of Hugging Face shows we do not seem to have reliable ways to curb extremely powerful AI systems

whitelist.title: Google says Gemini now has 950 million monthly users.
whitelist.desc: It’s a sizeable jump from the 750 million Gemini users Google reported in February. Alphabet, Google’s parent company, shared the stat as part of its Q2 2026 earnings report (pdf), which also revealed a 24 percent increase in revenue to $119.8 billion. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/google-q2-2026-earnings.png?quality=90&strip=all]

whitelist.title: Anthropic to buy up to 2 gigawatts of GPU capacity from AMD
whitelist.desc: Anthropic PBC will purchase up to 2 gigawatts' worth of graphics cards from Advanced Micro Devices Inc. as part of a multibillion-dollar deal announced today.The partnership also has several other

whitelist.title: OpenAI's models went rogue and hacked Hugging Face. More concerning behavior may be next | Fortune
whitelist.desc: AI safety researchers warn that smarter models are getting better at gaming the system to get what they want—and could start hiding real intentions altogether.

whitelist.title: White House steers $5 billion toward AI research in biggest federal science overhaul in 80 years
whitelist.desc: Federal agencies committed over $5 billion to the Genesis Mission, an Energy Department-led effort to use AI to accelerate research across 278 projects

whitelist.title: ‘Out-of-favor’ utilities could be the next beneficiaries of the AI trade – and they pay dividends, Wells Fargo says
whitelist.desc: A few overlooked names in the utilities sector could be poised for gains, the bank said.

whitelist.title: OpenAI's latest proposal shows it's very aware that data centers are unpopular
whitelist.desc: OpenAI's latest data center proposal, "Project Camellia," included pledges regarding electricity bills, water use, and Codex credits for students.

whitelist.title: 'Gemini who?': Rivals dunk on Google's delayed frontier AI
whitelist.desc: Google's delayed frontier model has rivals taking jabs. One analyst said it has shifted the perception "from leading edge to trailing edge."

whitelist.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

whitelist.title: China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook
whitelist.desc: As access to Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s frontier models becomes more restricted, Chinese labs are pitching their open-source alternatives as stable, accessible, and increasingly capable.

whitelist.title: OpenAI sued over ChatGPT health advice that almost killed a pastor - Engadget
whitelist.desc: ChatGPT allegedly offered "extremely dangerous medical recommendations" regarding a pulmonary embolism.

whitelist.title: Monday.com is cutting 20 percent of its workforce as it pivots to an AI work platform
whitelist.desc: Monday.com will lay off about 620 employees and flatten its management structure as the project management company chases what it calls the AI Work Platform

whitelist.title: How to use AI to make a graphical abstract in minutes
whitelist.desc: Artificial intelligence is a democratizing force for those without graphic-design experience, as long as it is used conscientiously.

whitelist.title: Three tips to avoid AI image mistakes in science
whitelist.desc: Artificial-intelligence tools can be useful for brainstorming and creating graphics, but they can introduce errors. If you’re going to use them, here’s how to avoid problems.

whitelist.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains what AI startups founders must do differently
whitelist.desc: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May. Menlo Ventures' Matt Murphy tells Equity it's the kind of growth curve he's never seen in 25 years of investing.

whitelist.title: This is the stock to buy after OpenAI's AI agent goes rogue in a cybersecurity test
whitelist.desc: Jim Cramer said CrowdStrike is the stock to buy after OpenAI's agentic breach.

whitelist.title: Arrakis emerges from stealth with $38 million to bring AI to factories and supply chains
whitelist.desc: London-based Arrakis raised $38M to build an AI operating system for industrial companies, betting that factories need AI more than office workers do

whitelist.title: China’s Moonshot trained its AI model on a restricted Nvidia chip, White House claims.
whitelist.desc: Michael Kratsios, the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy director, writes on X that Moonshot accessed Nvidia’s high-end GB300 processors in Thailand, which are subject to strict export controls. Kratsios also accuses Moonshot of distilling Anthropic’s Fable AI model to build Kimi K3, an open-source AI system claimed to rival flagship models from US companies. [Link: White House Official Says Moonshot Accessed Banned Nvidia Chips | https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-...

whitelist.title: YouTuber Gossip Goblin Will Debut AI Film In U.S. Theaters
whitelist.desc: YouTube creator Gossip Goblin brings AI-assisted feature "Gods Don’t Give Gifts" to U.S. theaters, testing whether online fandom can drive box office.

whitelist.title: Cisco’s tiny open-weight AI hunts bugs, beating Gemini
whitelist.desc: Cisco’s open-weight Antares models find code bugs on your own machines. It claims they beat Gemini and GPT-class systems, faster and far cheaper.

whitelist.title: Amazon is cutting jobs on its AGI team.
whitelist.desc: In a statement to Reuters, an Amazon spokesperson confirmed that it’s “eliminating some ‌roles ⁠within parts of our AGI [advanced general intelligence] organization” as the company focuses on “initiatives that matter most for customers.” It’s not clear how many workers are impacted. [Link: Amazon cuts jobs in its artificial general intelligence group | https://www.reuters.com/business/world-at-work/amazon-cuts-jobs-its-artificial-general-intelligence-group-2026-07-22/ | Reuters]

whitelist.title: Americans oppose AI data centres more than any building
whitelist.desc: A Redfin poll finds 53% of Americans oppose an AI data centre nearby, more than any other building. But in Virginia, they are quietly funding schools.

whitelist.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among US companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

whitelist.desc: Velocity, backed by AppLovin and a $27M seed round, is building ad-monetization infrastructure for AI apps, arguing intent-based advertising can fund free access where subscriptions fall short.
